topic.what: Machine-checked cardinality bounds for Barrett reduction under first-order arithmetic masking over prime fields; proves preimage cardinality trichotomy {0,1,2} for Barrett internal wire map
topic.how: Lean 4 + Mathlib kernel-verified proofs across 12 theorems with zero sorry; introduces PF-PINI framework as preimage multiplicity bound for prime-field gadgets
topic.why_matters: Closes verification gap for NTT-based PQC (ML-KEM/ML-DSA); provides universal 1-bit min-entropy bound applicable to any modulus without per-parameter re-verification; supports FIPS 140-3 certification arguments per NIST IR 8547

characteristics.what_it_measures: Information leakage per Barrett internal wire under first-order probing
characteristics.context: Universal over all q>0 and shift parameters s; count-zero cases make actual leakage strictly less than 1 bit often

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum number of masks producing any output value
characteristics.context: Tight: count=2 achievable when both branch candidates valid and r != 0

characteristics.evidence: Table 5 enumeration: 10 theorems + 2 verified gadget constructions
characteristics.context: Zero sorry, zero admit, zero native_decide calls

characteristics.what_it_measures: Generality of cardinality bound across moduli
characteristics.evidence: Lean theorems hold for any positive integer q; no primality requirement
characteristics.context: Scope condition s >= ceil(log2(q)) satisfied by all practical implementations; ML-KEM q=3329, ML-DSA q=8380417 covered

characteristics.what_it_measures: Min-entropy floor for ML-KEM Barrett wires
Min-entropy floor for ML-DSA Barrett wires

good_for: NTT pipeline first-order arithmetic masking composition
Universal leakage bounds across PQC standards
requires: Fresh per-stage masking for composition bound

novelty: Trichotomy theorem: Barrett internal wire preimage cardinality in {0,1,2}, never more, universal
PF-PINI framework: preimage multiplicity bound for prime-field masked gadgets
1-Bit Barrier: first universal machine-checked min-entropy bound for masked Barrett reduction
Support gap characterization: unreachable outputs reduce actual leakage below 1 bit
Adams Bridge failure mode: inter-stage masking absence formally characterized via PF-PINI violation
open_problems: Support gap formula: min(x.val+1, q-r, q-1-x.val) not yet proved in Lean
PF-PINI composition: max(k1,k2) bound with fresh masking is observation, not theorem
Information-theoretic formalization: H_infinity bound follows by standard definitions, not kernel-verified
Higher-order Barrett: d>=2 analysis requires joint preimage characterization across multiple wires
Nat equivalence bridge: barrettInternalMapNat equivalence to algebraic definition not proved
